refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR uses 'support achieving' which can be ambiguous and potentially implies a solution approach. Replacing it with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on the system's capability without prescribing how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single requirement related to energy efficiency target achievement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use
Design energy recovery mechanisms to reuse energy within the system
Establish predictive maintenance to ensure energy-efficient operation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

potential_sources: Feedback control tightly linked to energy measurement accuracy
Operational policies may influence control parameters
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical control loop that can be modularized independently from other system functions, supporting decoupling. Potential coupling arises if operational policies are embedded within the control logic rather than managed separately.

assumptions_risks: Energy measurement can be accurately and reliably obtained in real time
Feedback control algorithms can be tuned to meet diverse customer targets
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that conflict with control strategies
Risk that inaccurate energy data leads to suboptimal control and failure to meet targets
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify separation between control logic and operational policy enforcement to maintain modularity and reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the energy management and monitoring functionalities within the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive real-time energy consumption data and send feedback control signals.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with other system components and external services for monitoring and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as energy monitoring and feedback control logically belong here; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Contains physical energy consumption but control logic and monitoring likely spread across other modules, causing moderate diffusion.
Primarily focused on remote control, not energy monitoring; would cause high diffusion.
Safety may interact with energy control but does not contain the core DP; moderate diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the DP of real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control, minimizing diffusion and centralizing ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause minor coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid unintended diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target. The DP logically supports measuring and controlling energy use to meet the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw energy data acquisition may cause minor coupling.
Interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link must be well-defined to avoid unintended diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and centralized ownership of the DP. Other modules show moderate to high diffusion risks, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through telemetry data and network link interfaces that provide real-time energy consumption data and feedback control signals, enabling measurement against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and specific, minimizing diffusion and centralizing responsibility for energy monitoring and control.

recommended_revisions: Define clear interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize unintended diffusion and coupling.
Establish explicit data acquisition protocols with the Vehicle module to manage dependencies and reduce coupling risks.
Document verification procedures leveraging telemetry data to confirm energy efficiency targets are met.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP logically supports the FR, and the IM allocation shows high containment and low diffusion, indicating a well-defined and cohesive solution. The recommended revisions focus on interface contracts and verification documentation rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize the current level with these refinements rather than further decomposing, which could introduce complexity and risk.

revise.why: To define clear interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link, establish explicit data acquisition protocols with the Vehicle module, and document verification procedures to minimize diffusion and coupling risks and ensure verification feasibility.

assumptions: Telemetry and Network Link interfaces can be clearly defined and controlled.
Vehicle module cooperation for data acquisition is feasible and can be formalized.
Verification through telemetry data is reliable and sufficient for confirming energy efficiency targets.
open_risks: Potential minor coupling with Vehicle module may impact modularity and maintainability.
Unintended diffusion through interfaces if contracts are not strictly enforced.
evidence_needed: Formal interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link.
Data acquisition protocols with Vehicle module.
Verification procedure documentation and telemetry data demonstrating compliance with energy efficiency targets.
